title: eriogonum anemophilum
intro: Eriogonum anemophilum, also known as windmill pink or desert windmill, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is used to support respiratory health, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu, as well as to treat skin conditions and wounds. Eriogonum anemophilum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, and is also used in traditional medicine as a topical application to promote wound healing.
